reviews.review_msg: Really good prof. Records and uploads lectures, assignments are free participation marks, and informs students what to study before exams. He does seem a little disorganized, often changing the syllabus and cancelling assignments last min. But overall, a very reasonable prof, and doesn't make the course more difficult than it needs to be.
Amazing lectures, takes his time to explain things in detail and uploads lots of materials for students to use for assignments and the final.

The lectures were very enjoyable, and has brief pauses for notetaking. The class is 2 hours long, but the lecture segment is usually only half of that, with an optional discussion for the second half. Grading was fair, and the TA that I had was quite fun.

This was an extra credit course, and to say the least, I didn't enjoy it much. Topics were hard to wrap my head around; some were absurd. TA was alright, not really helpful, only went for the attendance mark. I'm personally not a writer, and if you are like me, a normal person, I would advise you not to take the class. Thomas is a good prof tho.

Provides very clear study guides, so nothing on exams feels unexpected. The grading rubric is explained well in advance. Exams are heavily weighted, but you can choose between multiple questions, which makes it feel fair. Very friendly and knowledgeable.
Makes PHIL110 bearable - lectures are easy to follow, and he uploads them online if you don't want to go in-person.
Really personable and articulate. Uploads his lectures but it's worth going in-person because he has in-class exercises that he likes to personally help students with
Incredible teacher -- just like all the other reviews say.  Has a striking resemblance to Mike Malott

Tom is very nice, and the TAs bring it even further by their textbook-level performance. Course content could be hard to understand at some times but Tom did try to clear it out and TAs provide extensive help during tutorials. Work gets heavy later in the semester, but 60-70% of the exams are basic questions and won't caught you in surprise.

straightforward class, 40% MT 50% Final, 10% participation. final was pretty tough. Tom assigns grades on relative performance, but if anything, thatll benefit you. The class was boring and potentially very confusing, but Tom is a gem of a prof, so kind and clear, and one of my favourites Ive had at SFU. Highly recommend taking this class w Tom!

Oh I loved attending this class. The professor and the TAs are super helpful, the class content itself is not too hard once you get the hang of it and spend time on it. 10/10 would love having a class like this again

A GEM OF A MAN. Tom cracks jokes that make you smile even on the most tiring of mornings and is cheery every lecture. I miss his enthusiasm for philosophy already, even as a non-philosophy major. He is very kind, eases you into 1st year well, and welcomes questions. TA's are terrific too. S tier prof, 0 complaints!

Tom is really nice and during every tutorial my TA goes over the lesson and gives us a handout to work on so that's really helpful. The only thing I would say is that Tom goes over the lecture slides really quickly and then ends up giving us a ton of time to do exercise questions, so sometimes it's hard to follow along.

Professor Donaldson was an amazing professor. He is so chill and nice, and his lectures are very entertaining and informative. He gives students opportunities for extra credit, and make up. He is also very caring and considerate, he will work with you if you are behind. This is at Florida Southwestern State College.

Tom is a caring prof, who is helpful and approachable. I would recommend going to office hours, he goes over his assignments and helps you answer the questions. Tutorials are mandatory and they take attendance but it's helpful so I don't mind going. His exams are similar to the practice exams, I would 100% recommend taking this class.

Lectures are very easy to follow along with & tutorials are super helpful in learning exactly what will be on the exam. Practice questions are super helpful as well. Thomas is very clear with teaching which makes it easy to understand.
Tom is very knowledgeable, the TA (Issac) was the GOAT. Phil isn't in my interest at all but once you understand the material it isn't bad at all, go to office hours!!!
He's an amazing person but the grading scale was mad weird.

Fantastic guy. 4.8/5 should be a 5/5.

Easy to get along with when I went to office hours. Completely understood alternate pathways to solve questions and even encouraged me to use them. 

He cares alot about what he does and it shows. Made the class very enjoyable! 

Would vouch 100% and highly recommend. Fantastic human.

Very direct and clear at explaining things. Very much cared about students learning and was very clear about what would be on the midterm and final and gave us resources to prepare. He also has a soothing voice with a British accent NGL. Deserving of the 4.8/5 rating as of current timing, should be 5/5.
HAS A BRITISH ACCENT !!!!

This class was a disaster. He rushes through material with no real explanations, leaving us completely lost. The TAs were no better—unhelpful and clueless about the course content. It felt like no one cared if we actually learned anything. If you value your time and education, avoid this class entirely.

Prof was very nice and easy. The TA's were also very fair markers. I would highly recommend to take this class. There are weekly assignments (easy 100%) and participation. Final paper and final exam were pretty easy too, you just have to remember repeated concepts.
Great prof, super easy to follow, engaging lectures. Assignments aren't too bad, but it depends on the TA. Easy gpa booster.

Tom was a fantastic professor! I went to the lectures just to hear him talk because he was so engaging and has a great accent. The grading is fair and assignments are pretty easy. He wrote the textbook, which is interesting but is not necessary to do well in the course. My TA (AJ) was super helpful in understanding the concepts. Highly recommend!

Truly awesome guy. Engaging lectures and clear feedback from TAs on assignments. Open to discussion and questions during class. Expect weekly assignments. He seems to really love what he does. Be aware that exact terminology for different beliefs is expected on the final-- don't be vague. Overall, great course taught by a great prof!
take him if u got the chance crodieeeeee

Literally the GOAT. My TA (AJ) was amazing - always provided good feedback and genuinely cared about us and wanted us to succeed. Tom is literally such an amazing prof and amazing person. Would 100% take another class with him. 10/10 as a dude and 10/10 as a prof.
Nothing against the prof, but horrible TA, ruined my whole grade and as long as you don't get her, you should be good.
Was an awesome professor and the lectures were very easy to follow and helped a lot

Tom is a really good prof. He is engaging and very effectively explains the topics. However, if you are put with a bad TA the class will be ruined for you. The class is based off personal opinions, but if your opinion is against what the TA wants then your wrong.

Tom is very engaging during lectures and takes a lot of questions. The weekly short questions are quite easy if you've done your readings beforehand. The first analytical assignment is short and easy, the second one is a bit longer but not too challenging.
He is exceptionally knowledgeable, so talk to him during his office hours.
Genuinely the textbook defenition of a good prof and a good person. He teaches well, makes sense, is clear, and conscise. you cant ask for more or better at sfu.
Overrated.          ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎
Very easy to follow. Attending the TA session helps a lot.

Took 110 in Fall 2021, but still the best prof I've met at SFU so far. Tom is a great example of what it means to be a good prof. You can tell he knows his material, loves what he's doing and is good at what he does. Expect engaging lectures with humour, personal stories and fun puzzles. The course content is kinda difficult, but Tom makes it easy.

Dr. Donaldson is a great prof! This course is a bit hard to understand but do the readings & you'll be fine. Lectures help you understand & very interesting topics as well! There are weekly assignments, 2 papers, and a final. Tutorials were fun too because the discussions were interesting & TA explained well. Great prof & class. Highly recommend!

Tom is a really chill prof and the content is pretty interesting. When it gets a bit more confusing and heavier, later on, he does a great job of reviewing and clarifying anything. PHIL100W is a pretty simple course and can help your GPA if you put in the work and effort.

Tom is an INCREDIBLE professor! This was his first time teaching Epistemology, so there were some hiccups along the way (i.e. scrapping our second paper). Regardless, Tom was always passionate, engaged, and accommodating. He communicates his expectations very clearly and ALWAYS puts the needs of his students first! I cannot recommend him enough!
